creator: osharoiko@gmail.com
text: Another workaround is to switch to netgraph. I have eventually did that (ng_bridge, ng_ether, ng_eiface). I had to write some startup scripts to manage netgraph topology but my system has been very stable since I have done that. I'm running with LACP, vlans, bridging, CARP and PF on amd64 and rpi3 (as a backup). I cannot say much for the performance of this setup - it seems to be fine for my home needs but I haven't tried load testing it. Not talking about performance of Pi obviously.
